ucs.title: [LLM] Flax Typhoon Sticky Keys / Accessibility Feature debugger hijack via IFEO registry
ucs.description: Hunts for Flax Typhoon's signature persistence + bypass technique: writing a 'Debugger' value under Image File Execution Options for an accessibility binary (sethc.exe, utilman.exe, magnify.exe, etc.) so that pressing Shift x5 / WinKey+U at the lock screen spawns SYSTEM-level cmd.exe. Microsoft Threat Intelligence (Aug 2023) called this the actor's most distinctive fingerprint after observed campaigns vs. Taiwanese government and academia.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\*" AND Registry.registry_value_name="Debugger" AND (Registry.registry_path="*\\sethc.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\utilman.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\magnify.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\narrator.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\osk.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\displayswitch.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\atbroker.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\hkcmd.exe*") AND Registry.user!="*$" by Registry.dest Registry.user Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data Registry.process_name Registry.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | rename dest as host, user as src_user | eval reason="Flax Typhoon style accessibility-feature IFEO debugger hijack"
ucs.kql:
// Flax Typhoon — accessibility-feature IFEO debugger hijack
// Microsoft (Aug 2023): sethc.exe / utilman.exe Debugger value swapped to cmd.exe or powershell.exe
let _accessibility_bins = dynamic(["sethc.exe","utilman.exe","magnify.exe","narrator.exe","osk.exe","displayswitch.exe","atbroker.exe","hkcmd.exe"]);
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has @"\Image File Execution Options\"
| where RegistryValueName =~ "Debugger"
| extend HijackedBinary = tolower(extract(@"(?i)Image File Execution Options\\([^\\]+)$", 1, RegistryKey))
| where HijackedBinary in~ (_accessibility_bins)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
   or InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("trustedinstaller.exe","msiexec.exe")  // exclude only known-good system installers
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, HijackedBinary,
          DebuggerSetTo = RegistryValueData,
          RegistryKey,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessAccountDomain, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel, IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession = tostring(InitiatingProcessAccountSid)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Flax Typhoon — accessibility-feature IFEO debugger hijack on Sentinel
// Catches both the registry write itself and the post-trigger SYSTEM cmd.exe / powershell.exe spawn under winlogon
let _accessibility_bins = dynamic(["sethc.exe","utilman.exe","magnify.exe","narrator.exe","osk.exe","displayswitch.exe","atbroker.exe","hkcmd.exe"]);
let _hijack_writes = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4657                                  // registry value set (must enable subcat)
    | where ObjectName has @"\Image File Execution Options\"
    | where ObjectValueName =~ "Debugger"
    | extend HijackedBinary = tolower(extract(@"(?i)Image File Execution Options\\([^\\]+)$", 1, ObjectName))
    | where HijackedBinary in~ (_accessibility_bins)
    | where SubjectUserName !endswith "$"
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, SubjectUserName, SubjectDomainName,
              HijackedBinary, RegistryKey = ObjectName, ProcessName;
let _post_trigger = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith @"\winlogon.exe"
    | where NewProcessName endswith_cs @"\cmd.exe" or NewProcessName endswith_cs @"\powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith_cs @"\pwsh.exe"
    | where TargetUserSid in ("S-1-5-18")                    // SYSTEM cmd from logon screen
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, TargetUserName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
union _hijack_writes, _post_trigger
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Flax Typhoon Accessibility Feature IFEO Debugger Hijack
id: c8e3a9c2-4d5f-4b1f-9a52-fla x-typhoon-ifeo-001
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of Image File Execution Options Debugger value for Windows accessibility binaries (sethc.exe, utilman.exe, etc.) — the signature persistence/bypass used by Flax Typhoon (CN/state) per Microsoft Threat Intelligence Aug 2023.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/08/24/flax-typhoon-using-legitimate-software-to-quietly-access-taiwanese-organizations/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1546.008
  - attack.privilege_escalation
  - threat.flax_typhoon
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: registry_set
detection:
  selection_path:
    TargetObject|contains: '\Image File Execution Options\'
    Details|contains: 'Debugger'
  selection_binary:
    TargetObject|endswith:
      - '\sethc.exe\Debugger'
      - '\utilman.exe\Debugger'
      - '\magnify.exe\Debugger'
      - '\narrator.exe\Debugger'
      - '\osk.exe\Debugger'
      - '\displayswitch.exe\Debugger'
      - '\atbroker.exe\Debugger'
      - '\hkcmd.exe\Debugger'
  filter_trustedinstaller:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
  condition: selection_binary and not filter_trustedinstaller
falsepositives:
  - Vendor accessibility-software installers (rare; verify Image is signed)
level: high
ucs.rationale: This is the literal fingerprint Microsoft attributed to Flax Typhoon when naming the cluster (also tracked as Ethereal Panda). The actor specifically targets accessibility binaries because they're invoked by winlogon at the lock screen, granting NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM without needing valid credentials. Generic IFEO detection rules fire on dev tooling (Visual Studio, debuggers); narrowing to the acces
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Flax Typhoon SoftEther VPN bridge persistence (vpnbridge / vpnserver / vpnclient)
ucs.description: Hunts deployment and execution of SoftEther VPN client/bridge binaries — Flax Typhoon's hallmark long-haul C2 / RMM-substitute. Microsoft observed the actor running SoftEther's vpnbridge.exe (often renamed conhost.exe / dllhost.exe) as a service to bridge victim networks back to attacker-controlled SoftEther hubs, replacing traditional implant C2 entirely.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("vpnbridge.exe","vpnserver.exe","vpnclient.exe","vpncmd.exe","vpnsmgr.exe","vpncmgr.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*BridgeCreate*","*HubCreate*","*CascadeCreate*","*AccountConnect*","*AccountStart*","*SoftEther*","* /HUB *","* /HUBLINK *","* /CMD *vpncmd*")) AND Processes.user!="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | append [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_port IN (443,992,5555,1194,8080,8443) AND All_Traffic.app="softether") OR All_Traffic.process_name IN ("vpnbridge.exe","vpnserver.exe","vpnclient.exe") by All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_ip All_Traffic.dest_port All_Traffic.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`] | eval reason="Flax Typhoon SoftEther VPN bridge tradecraft"
ucs.kql:
// Flax Typhoon — SoftEther VPN bridge installation / execution
// Catches both the canonical binary names AND renamed copies (via VersionInfo) AND the cmd flags Flax uses
let _softether_filenames = dynamic(["vpnbridge.exe","vpnserver.exe","vpnclient.exe","vpncmd.exe","vpnsmgr.exe","vpncmgr.exe"]);
let _softether_cmd_markers = dynamic(["BridgeCreate","HubCreate","CascadeCreate","AccountConnect","AccountStart","AccountCreate","/HUB:","/HUBLINK","vpncmd /SERVER","vpncmd /CLIENT","SoftEther"]);
let _process_hits = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName in~ (_softether_filenames)
        or ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has "SoftEther"
        or ProcessVersionInfoProductName has "SoftEther"
        or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName in~ (_softether_filenames)
        or ProcessCommandLine has_any (_softether_cmd_markers)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
              OriginalName = ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName,
              Company = ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
              Product = ProcessVersionInfoProductName,
              ProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              EvidenceType = "ProcessExec";
let _service_install = DeviceEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("ServiceInstalled","ScheduledTaskCreated")
    | where AdditionalFields has_any ("vpnbridge","vpnserver","vpnclient","SoftEther")
          or FolderPath has_any ("vpnbridge","vpnserver","SoftEther")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              EvidenceType = strcat("Service:",ActionType);
union _process_hits, _service_install
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Flax Typhoon — SoftEther VPN bridge on Sentinel (4688 + ASIM fallback for cross-vendor)
let _softether_filenames = dynamic(["vpnbridge.exe","vpnserver.exe","vpnclient.exe","vpncmd.exe","vpnsmgr.exe","vpncmgr.exe"]);
let _softether_cmd_markers = dynamic(["BridgeCreate","HubCreate","CascadeCreate","AccountConnect","AccountStart","AccountCreate","/HUB:","/HUBLINK","SoftEther"]);
union isfuzzy=true
    ( SecurityEvent
        | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
        | where EventID == 4688
        | where Account !endswith "$"
        | where NewProcessName has_any (_softether_filenames)
            or CommandLine has_any (_softether_cmd_markers)
        | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
                  NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName,
                  EvidenceType = "SecurityEvent4688" ),
    ( ImProcessCreate
        | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
        | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
        | where TargetProcessName has_any (_softether_filenames)
            or ActingProcessFileCompany has "SoftEther"
            or TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (_softether_cmd_markers)
        | project TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname, Account = ActorUsername,
                  NewProcessName = TargetProcessName,
                  CommandLine = TargetProcessCommandLine,
                  ParentProcessName,
                  EvidenceType = "ASIM:ImProcessCreate" ),
    ( Event
        | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
        | where Source == "Service Control Manager" and EventID == 7045   // service install
        | where RenderedDescription has_any ("vpnbridge","vpnserver","vpnclient","SoftEther")
        | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account = UserName,
                  NewProcessName = "", CommandLine = RenderedDescription,
                  ParentProcessName = "",
                  EvidenceType = "ServiceInstall:7045" )
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: SoftEther VPN bridge is uncommon in legitimate enterprise networks and is THE Flax Typhoon C2 substitute called out in Microsoft's 2023 attribution report and CrowdStrike's Ethereal Panda profile. The detection looks at three independent anchors — exact binary names, signed-but-renamed binaries (via VersionInfo), and the SoftEther vpncmd CLI verbs (BridgeCreate, HubCreate, CascadeCreate) — so even
